How to Play — Dungeon Solitaire

Pull the New Game lever to begin; you become the active player. Your allies appear and line up before you. Five columns of foes stand ahead, most face-down — examine any figure to read its card.

Each turn, CLICK one of your allies, then choose which enemy column it strikes. Defeat a front enemy to reveal the next behind it. Some allies unleash their power on the whole dungeon instead of attacking one column.

Clear every column to WIN. Run out of allies while foes remain and you LOSE. Your score is the cards you have left (win) or the foes that remain (loss).

Only the active player may act; others may watch. Leave the area for 30 seconds and the board clears for the next challenger.

Dungeon Solitaire — Credits

A solitaire dungeon-crawl card game, themed on Middle-earth for Homer's Quest.

-- GAME DESIGN --
Steven Hastings
Conceived the game, playtested it on paper, and designed every card, ability, and rule. Design consultant throughout the digital development.

-- DIGITAL DEVELOPMENT (engine) --
James Price
Core engine architecture, all card-effect implementations, and testing of the digital game (DungeonSolitaire.Core, C#).

-- NWN INTEGRATION --
James Price (Homer's Quest)
Claude (Anthropic) — the in-process Anvil / NWNX:EE plugin (DungeonSolitaire.Nwn) that renders the board with the module's creatures and statues, the attack and choice conversations, and the narrator that speaks the game.

-- AI ASSISTANCE (engine) --
ChatGPT: early development guidance and initial skeleton framework.
Claude AI: GameEventBus event system, input command pattern (RequestInput / SubmitInput), engine refactoring, and save / replay system.

Cards are portrayed by the module's creatures; missing characters use a stand-in.

Dungeon Solitaire — High Scores

No champions yet. Pull the lever and be the first!

New Game Lever

Pull this lever to start a new game of Dungeon Solitaire. You become the active player until you win, lose, or leave the area. Pull it again during your game to restart.
The marshalling ground where your allies arrive before each game of Dungeon Solitaire.
